textEn: By those [angels] who extract with violence
And [by] those who remove with ease
And [by] those who glide [as if] swimming
And those who race each other in a race
And those who arrange [each] matter,
On the Day the blast [of the Horn] will convulse [creation],
There will follow it the subsequent [one].
Hearts, that Day, will tremble,
Their eyes humbled.
They are [presently] saying, "Will we indeed be returned to [our] former state [of life]?
textTranslit: Ya qoo loona a-inna lamar doo doona fil haafirah

textEn: Even if we should be decayed bones?"
They say, "That, then, would be a losing return."
Indeed, it will be but one shout,
And suddenly they will be [alert] upon the earth's surface.
Has there reached you the story of Moses? -

textAr: إِذْ نَادَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًى
textEn: When his Lord called to him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā,
textTranslit: Iz nadaahu rabbuhu bil waadil-muqad dasi tuwa

textEn: "Go to Pharaoh. Indeed, he has transgressed.

textAr: فَقُلْ هَل لَّكَ إِلَىٰٓ أَن تَزَكَّىٰ
textEn: And say to him, 'Would you [be willing to] purify yourself
And let me guide you to your Lord so you would fear [Him]?'"
textTranslit: Wa ahdi yaka ila rabbika fatakh sha

textEn: And he showed him the greatest sign,
But he [i.e., Pharaoh] denied and disobeyed.
Then he turned his back, striving [i.e., plotting].
And he gathered [his people] and called out.
And said, "I am your most exalted lord."
So Allāh seized him in exemplary punishment for the last and the first [transgression].1.
textTranslit: Fa-akha zahul laahu nakalal aakhirati wal-oola.

textAr: إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّمَن يَخْشَىٰٓ
textEn: Indeed in that is a lesson [i.e., warning] for whoever would fear [Allāh].

textAr: ءَأَنتُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقًا أَمِ ٱلسَّمَآءُ ۚ بَنَىٰهَا
textEn: Are you a more difficult creation or is the heaven? He [i.e., Allāh] constructed it.
textTranslit: A-antum a shaddu khalqan amis samaa-u banaaha.

textEn: He raised its ceiling and proportioned it.
And He darkened its night and extracted its brightness.
textTranslit: Wa aghtasha lailaha wa akhraja duhaaha.

textEn: And after that He spread the earth.
He extracted from it its water and its pasture,
And the mountains He set firmly
As enjoyment [i.e., provision] for you and your grazing livestock.
But when there comes the greatest Overwhelming Calamity -
The Day when man will remember that for which he strove,
textTranslit: Yauma Yata zakkarul insaanu ma sa'aa.

textEn: And Hellfire will be exposed for [all] those who see -
So as for he who transgressed
And preferred the life of the world,
Then indeed, Hellfire will be [his] refuge.

textAr: وَأَمَّا مَنْ خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِۦ وَنَهَى ٱلنَّفْسَ عَنِ ٱلْهَوَىٰ
textEn: But as for he who feared the position of his Lord and prevented the soul from [unlawful] inclination,
textTranslit: Wa ammaa man khaafa maqaama Rabbihee wa nahan nafsa 'anil hawaa

textEn: Then indeed, Paradise will be [his] refuge.
They ask you, [O Muḥammad], about the Hour: when is its arrival?
In what [position] are you that you should mention it?
To your Lord is its finality.
You are only a warner for those who fear it.

textAr: كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَهَا لَمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَوْ ضُحَىٰهَا
textEn: It will be, on the Day they see it, as though they had not remained [in the world] except for an afternoon or a morning thereof.
textTranslit: Ka annahum Yawma yarawnahaa lam yalbasooo illaa 'ashiyyatan aw duhaahaa
